moon_deities.name: Sin
moon_deities.description: Sin, also known as Nanna, is the Mesopotamian god of the moon, associated with wisdom and the calendar.

moon_deities.name: Nanna
moon_deities.description: Nanna is the Sumerian god of the moon, father of Utu (sun god) and Inanna (goddess of love and war).

moon_deities.name: Khonsu
moon_deities.description: Khonsu is the Egyptian god of the moon, time, and healing, often depicted with a sidelock of youth and a lunar disk.

moon_deities.name: Iah
moon_deities.description: Iah is an ancient Egyptian lunar deity, sometimes merged with Thoth and Khonsu.

moon_deities.name: Selene
moon_deities.description: Selene is the Greek goddess of the Moon, often depicted driving a silver chariot across the night sky.

moon_deities.name: Artemis
moon_deities.description: Artemis is the goddess of the hunt, wilderness, and the moon, associated with chastity and protection.

moon_deities.name: Hecate
moon_deities.description: Hecate is a Greek goddess associated with magic, witchcraft, the night, and the waning moon.

moon_deities.name: Luna
moon_deities.description: Luna is the Roman goddess of the moon, equivalent to the Greek Selene.

moon_deities.name: Diana
moon_deities.description: Diana is the Roman goddess of the hunt and the moon, protector of women and children.

moon_deities.name: Chandra
moon_deities.description: Chandra is the Hindu moon god, associated with the night, vegetation, and fertility.

moon_deities.name: Soma
moon_deities.description: Soma is the Vedic deity of the moon and the personification of the sacred soma plant.

moon_deities.name: Tsukuyomi
moon_deities.description: Tsukuyomi is the Japanese moon god, born from Izanagi, representing order and night.

moon_deities.name: Chang'e
moon_deities.description: Chang'e is the Chinese moon goddess who lives on the moon after consuming an elixir of immortality.

moon_deities.name: Yue Lao
moon_deities.description: Yue Lao is the Chinese god of marriage and love, associated with the moon and red thread of fate.

moon_deities.name: Coyolxauhqui
moon_deities.description: Coyolxauhqui is the Aztec moon goddess, depicted as a dismembered warrior, symbolizing the moon's phases.

moon_deities.name: Tecciztecatl
moon_deities.description: Tecciztecatl is the Aztec moon god, associated with the creation of the moon and bravery.

moon_deities.name: Ix Chel
moon_deities.description: Ix Chel is the Mayan goddess of the moon, fertility, medicine, and weaving.

moon_deities.name: Mama Quilla
moon_deities.description: Mama Quilla is the Incan goddess of the moon and marriage, protector of women.

moon_deities.name: Máni
moon_deities.description: Máni is the Norse personification of the moon, who guides the moon across the sky in a chariot.

moon_deities.name: Cerridwen
moon_deities.description: Cerridwen is a Welsh goddess of the moon, wisdom, and transformation, keeper of the cauldron of knowledge.

moon_deities.name: Rhiannon
moon_deities.description: Rhiannon is a Welsh goddess associated with the moon, horses, and the Otherworld.
